notes: Task #9 iter 2 BREAKTHROUGH candidate FAILED — same outcome shape as #1183. 3-frame NDJSON envelope (patch-start + patch agent-inference + record-map with thread + thread_message) did NOT produce a rendered chat bubble. Test execution: notion.com/chat hard reload → fresh thread URL ?t=3659aa2d8bdd80cb909b00a9690ed8ac → typed 주민등록번호 prompt → submitted → 120s wait. Engine intercept fired: Network panel filtered by 'runInference' shows 2 Fetch runInferenceTranscript captures (rows at y=420 and y=446) after submit, confirming the c2s POST was intercepted and the response envelope was returned to the FE. However, the sidebar Chat history menu still shows only pre-existing entries ('Simple addition question 19m', 'New AI chat 1w' x3) — NO new thread title appeared for the just-submitted prompt within 120s. Composer cleared (submit succeeded), thread URL changed (FE created a thread record locally), but no assistant response bubble was committed to the visible chat surface. Accessibility tree exposes no chat thread content under the Notion AI focused window, identical to #1183 capture. The 3-frame multi-frame approach (mirroring m365 iter5 dc5ab63 pattern) did not solve the FE state-machine commit issue: notion's runInferenceTranscript reconciliation likely requires additional frame types beyond patch-start/patch/record-map — possibly a 'completed'/'finalized' status frame on the agent-inference step, OR additional clock/version metadata in the macros (spaceId/threadId/traceId) extracted from c2s, OR a sibling collection_view/block_create frame for the thread_message. Recommend Phase 3: capture a successful natural notion response HAR with engine OFF and diff full frame sequence + per-frame headers + clock fields against current envelope to identify the missing trigger element. The runInferenceTranscript request was observed but Response-tab raw NDJSON content was not byte-verified from main session (32MB protection); 200/x-ndjson/3-line assertion is inferred from engine spec, not visually validated.
